message:
feat(suggestions): register tar-remove-files rule suggestions and pin in test

Follow-up to bd7970a (tar --remove-files destructive coverage) — wires
the two new rule ids into the suggestions registry so block messages
ship with actionable next-step suggestions instead of bare error
text.

Changes in `register_core_filesystem_suggestions`:

- `core.filesystem:tar-remove-files-root-home` →
  `rm_rf_suggestions.clone()`
- `core.filesystem:tar-remove-files-general` → `rm_rf_suggestions`
  (final move; everything else above clones)

Both reuse the same `rm_rf_suggestions` set as the rest of the
recursive-delete-equivalents (rm-rf-*, find-delete-*, unlink-*,
truncate-zero-*, shred-*). The safer alternatives — preview with
`ls`, scope to /tmp, use trash-cli or `rm -ri` — apply identically
across all six destruction primitives. Adds the existing
`shred-general` insert to use `clone()` so the move can land on
`tar-remove-files-general` instead.

Also extends the registry-coverage test
(`test_register_core_filesystem_suggestions`) to require the two
new rule ids in the expected list. The test guarantees that any
future destructive rule added to `core.filesystem` either has
suggestions registered or fails the test — exactly what we want
when the rule alternation in filesystem.rs is the source of truth
and the suggestions registry is the duplicate-truth that has to
keep up.
